definitions.text: NATO ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
The number represented by the Arabic numeral 1; the numerical value equal to that cardinal number.
examples: In some religions, there is more than one god.
In many cultures, a baby turns one year old a year after its birth.

definitions.text: The first positive number in the set of natural numbers.
The cardinality of the smallest nonempty set.
One thing (among a group of others); one member of a group.
examples: Any one of the boys.
Every one of the bank’s employees.

definitions.text: The first mentioned of two things or people, as opposed to the other.
examples: She offered him an apple and an orange; he took (the) one and left the other.
I am one of the few women who've climbed Everest.

definitions.text: Used to briefly refer to a noun phrase understood by context A one-dollar bill.
examples: I need some ones to make change.
Did you hear the one about the agnostic dyslexic insomniac?
I need some ones to make change.

definitions.text: Of a period of time, being particular.
examples: One day the prince set forth to kill the dragon that had brought terror to his father’s kingdom for centuries.

definitions.text: Being a single, unspecified thing; a; any.
examples: My aunt used to say, "One day is just like the other."
He is the one man who can help you.
The one male audience member at the concert is invited on stage.
There was one box of biscuits available.

definitions.text: Used for emphasis in place of a Being a preeminent example.
examples: He is one hell of a guy.
The town records from 1843 showed the overnight incarceration of one “A. Lincoln”.
He is one hell of a guy.

definitions.text: To cause to become one; to gather into a single whole; to unite.
Used at the end of a sentence to highlight the characteristics of someone or something.
examples: Got almonds one.
How come so heavy one ah?

definitions.text: Used as a relative pronoun at the end of a relative clause.
examples: The bird that make a lot of noise one is gone already.
